leadText: The Rage Candy Bar (Japanese: いかりまんじゅう Rage Manjū), formatted as RageCandyBar as an item prior to Generation VI, is a type of medicine introduced in the Generation II games. In Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver, it is a Key Item. It is a local specialty of Mahogany Town.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation II 1.2 Generation IV 1.3 Generations V and VI 1.4 Generation VII onward Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation II [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it heals the Pokémon by 20 HP , identical in behavior to a Potion . Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver , it may be exchanged for TM64 ( Explosion ) with an NPC on the Underground Path between Routes 5 and 6 . Generations V and VI [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it heals the Pokémon by 20 HP . In Pokémon Black and White , if the player uses a RageCandyBar on one of the Darmanitan statues in the Desert Resort , the RageCandyBar is consumed and will prompt a battle with the awakened Darmanitan. Each Darmanitan will be at level 35 and have their Hidden Ability , Zen Mode . Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it cures a Pokémon of all non-volatile status conditions ( poison , paralysis , sleep , burn , freeze ) and confusion .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description G S C Restores Pok é mon HP by 20. HG SS A famous candy in Mahogany Town. Many tourists like to buy them to take home. B W B2 W2 A famous Mahogany Town candy tourists like to buy and take home. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 20 points. X Y OR AS Mahogany Town's famous candy. When consumed, it restores 20 HP to an injured Pokémon. S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA Mahogany Town's famous candy. It can be used once to heal all the status conditions of a Pokémon. S V Mahogany Town's famous candy. It can be used to cure any status condition a Pokémon may have.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Though the item does not exist in the internal data of the Generation III and Sinnoh -based Generation IV games, it is mentioned in those games: In Pokémon FireRed and LeafGreen , it is mentioned by an NPC in the town on Two Island , who claims that is the favorite delicacy of Bruno of the Elite Four , who left the island in disappointment when he discovered that the local shop was sold out of them. In Pokémon Platinum , it is supposed to be sold at Veilstone Department Store 's basement, but the vendor selling them always says they're sold out of them. They are also said to be favored by Professor Rowan , who can occasionally be found in the Department Store basement, sulking about the fact that the store has run out of them. An NPC in the basement claims to be the one who has bought all the available Rage Candy Bars.
